---
title: "analysis"
author: "Tiantian Li"
date: "2026-03-06"
output: html_document
---

```{r, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE, warning= FALSE}
library(here)
joined_clean <- read.csv(here("data", "joined_clean.csv"))

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
```

# Step 3.1: Compute taxable income and tax liability at the bracket level
```{r}
joined_clean <- joined_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # Apply personal exemption (deduction) BEFORE tax calculation
    income_after_personal_exemption =
      median_income - personal_exemption_clean,
    
    # Temporary alias for readability in conditional logic
    wage = median_income,
    
    # Compute tax contribution from each bracket using progressive logic
    tax_in_bracket = case_when(
      # If wage does not reach the lower bound of this bracket, no tax in this bracket
      wage <= lower ~ 0,
      
      # Top bracket (upper is NA): tax the portion above lower bound
      is.na(upper) ~ (wage - lower) * rate,
      
      # Wage falls inside this bracket: tax the portion above lower bound
      wage < upper ~ (wage - lower) * rate,
      
      # Wage exceeds this bracket: fully tax this bracket range
      wage >= upper ~ (upper - lower) * rate,
      
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # Safety check: if any negative values appear (should not happen), set them to NA
    tax_in_bracket = if_else(tax_in_bracket < 0, NA_real_, tax_in_bracket)
  ) %>%
  select(-wage)  # Drop temporary helper column
```

# Step 3.2: Aggregate bracket-level taxes to state-level total tax

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r}
joined_clean <- joined_clean %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  mutate(
    # Sum tax across all brackets for each state
    total_tax_by_state = sum(tax_in_bracket, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Apply tax credit AFTER tax is computed
    tax_after_credit = total_tax_by_state - credit,
    
    # Compute net income after tax
    net_income = median_income - tax_after_credit
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

# Step 3.3: Create a state-level summary table (one row per state)



# Step 3.4: Load rental cost data and combine it with net income
```{r}
state_rental <- read.csv(here("data", "rental.csv"))
income_rental <- joined_clean %>%
  left_join(state_rental, by = "state") %>%
  select(state, net_income, AverageRent_1BR_usd_2025)
```

```{r}
grocery <- read.csv(here("data", "grocery-prices-by-state-2026.csv"))
state_summary <- joined_clean %>%
  left_join(state_rental, by = "state") %>%
  left_join(grocery, by = "state") %>%
  mutate(remaining_income = net_income - AverageRent_1BR_usd_2025 * 12 - GroceryPricesPerWeek_2024) %>%
  select(state, net_income, AverageRent_1BR_usd_2025, GroceryPricesPerWeek_2024, remaining_income) %>%
  distinct()

```

```{r}
state_summary
